title: Fix: board worktree setup refuses to fall back to LOCAL kanban/board branch when…
prompt:
BUG (confirmed in the field): A project becomes invisible / its board fails to load because the board-branch-decoupling startup migration refuses to proceed when 'git fetch origin kanban/board' fails — even though a fully-populated LOCAL 'kanban/board' branch already exists.

REPRO / FIELD CASE:
- Kanban was first run under systemd; it completed board decoupling for a repo (repoPath e.g. /home/developer/code/zx-project-code) — created a LOCAL 'kanban/board' branch with the board data — but the branch was NEVER pushed to origin.
- Later the user started Kanban directly. On startup, createBoardWorktree → setupBoardWorktree → migrateDecoupleBoardToBranch runs 'git fetch origin kanban/board'. origin has no such branch, so the fetch fails.
- The code then throws: 'Could not fetch the board branch kanban/board from origin (the remote may be unreachable or slow). Refusing to initialize an empty board over data that may live on the remote.' It falls back to leaving data on the code branch and RETRIES every ~30s, spamming ERROR logs.
- Net effect: the project's board never materializes and the project is not visible in the UI, despite the data being safe on the LOCAL kanban/board branch.

ROOT-CAUSE REASONING: The fetch-from-origin precondition exists to avoid initializing an EMPTY board that would shadow real board data living only on the remote. That safety check is correct when there is NO local board branch. But when a LOCAL 'kanban/board' branch already exists and contains data, refusing to attach it is wrong — there is nothing to clobber and no ambiguity. The fix must distinguish 'no local branch + fetch failed (genuinely unsafe → refuse)' from 'local branch already exists (safe → use it, fetch is best-effort)'.

WHERE TO LOOK (compiled paths in dist/cli.js → map to source):
- createBoardWorktree / setupBoardWorktree / migrateDecoupleBoardToBranch in the board worktree git-ops layer (src/workspace/board-worktree.ts and the board-sync / workspace-state migration path; see memory 'board-branch-decoupling-p5-implemented'). Also board-ref handling (src/state/board-ref.ts).

REQUIRED FIX:
- Before (or after) the 'git fetch origin <branch>' attempt, check whether the branch exists LOCALLY (git rev-parse --verify / git show-ref). 
- If a local 'kanban/board' branch exists: treat the origin fetch as BEST-EFFORT — proceed to 'git worktree add' using the local branch; do NOT throw, do NOT refuse, do NOT loop. (Optionally log a warning that the branch is not yet on origin and board sync push will publish it.)
- Only keep the hard 'refuse to init empty board' behavior for the genuinely unsafe case: no local branch AND fetch failed (can't tell whether remote has data). 
- Make sure the existing retry loop stops once the worktree is successfully attached from the local branch (no more 30s ERROR spam).
- Preserve the non-destructive guarantees and the conflict-surfacing behavior already in board-sync (memory notes: merge --abort on real conflict, never auto-resolve).

ACCEPTANCE:
- With a local kanban/board branch present and origin lacking it (or origin unreachable), startup attaches the board worktree from the LOCAL branch, the project + board load, and no repeating ERROR is logged.
- The empty-board safety still triggers when there is NO local branch and the fetch fails.
- Unit/integration test covering: (a) local branch exists + fetch fails → worktree attached from local; (b) no local branch + fetch fails → still refuses.
- typecheck + biome/lint clean.
